title: Machaerina nukuhivensis
taxonomy: Machaerina nukuhivensis is classified within the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. This family consists of approximately 5,000 species of grass-like plants, including sedges and saw-sedges. Machaerina nukuhivensis is further classified within the genus Machaerina, which contains about 70 species of sedge plants. The species is native to the Pacific Islands and is known for its unique characteristics and adaptations. The classification of Machaerina nukuhivensis into its kingdom, phylum, class, and order is as follows: kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Liliopsida, and order Poales.
